summary: Iteration 29 — Cancel Pipeline + Startup Recovery testing. Backend 11/11 tests pass. Found and fixed TOCTOU race condition bug in pipeline.py. Found and fixed 3 frontend bugs (auto-resume field mismatch, missing pipelineAgentStatus init). Cancel button visible and clickable. Interrupted/cancelled toasts working. One minor double-toast race condition remains.

minor.issue: TOCTOU race condition: find_one → if status==running → update_one pattern could overwrite a 'cancelled' status if cancel API ran between the read and write. FIXED with atomic: update_one({id, status:'running'}, {$set:{status:'completed'}}).

ui_bugs.issue: latestRun.run_id used but GET /api/pipeline/run/project/latest returns 'id' field (not 'run_id'). Cancel button never appeared on page reload. FIXED: latestRun.run_id || latestRun.id
Missing setPipelineAgentStatus(latestRun.agent_status) on auto-resume. Progress bar and cancel button wouldn't show until first poll (4s delay). FIXED.
Server-side pipeline start (line 788) didn't call setPipelineAgentStatus. Cancel button showed only after first poll (4s). FIXED: setPipelineAgentStatus(initialStatus) now called immediately.

integration_issues.flow: Cancel pipeline - stale badges after cancel
integration_issues.issue: After cancel, if a poll was in-flight, setPipelineAgentStatus from the in-flight poll may re-populate agent badges. activePipelineId is cleared (cancel btn gone) but badges appear without cancel btn. Minor cosmetic issue.

When polling detects 'interrupted' terminal status, occasionally fires twice (an in-flight poll completes just before interval is cleared), showing duplicate 'Pipeline was interrupted' warning toasts. Minor UX issue.

action_items: MINOR: Fix double-toast race condition in polling: when terminal status detected, add clearInterval(interval) INSIDE the callback before calling state setters, to prevent in-flight duplicate poll from also detecting terminal status.

MINOR: After cancelPipeline(), setPipelineAgentStatus({}) is called but an in-flight poll might restore it. Consider using a useRef cancel flag inside the polling useEffect to guard against stale updates.

INFO: Startup handler verified running (server starts cleanly, health check OK). 0 interrupted pipelines in DB because test pipelines complete in ~4s - no pipeline was 'running' during any server restart. Code logic is correct.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: TOCTOU race in _execute_server_pipeline - now uses atomic conditional update_one({id, status:'running'}) instead of find_one + if running + update_one.
FIXED: Frontend auto-resume had wrong field name (run_id vs id). API GET returns 'id' but POST returns 'run_id'. Auto-resume now uses run_id || id.
FIXED: pipelineAgentStatus not initialized on auto-resume or server-side pipeline start - both now set it immediately so cancel button shows without waiting for first poll.
NOTE: Cancel endpoint correctly returns {success:true, run_id, status:'cancelled'} with HTTP 200.
NOTE: Cancel on non-running pipeline correctly returns 400 with detail message.
NOTE: startup handler in server.py correctly marks 'running' → 'interrupted' with update_many on startup.
NOTE: Frontend polling correctly handles all 4 terminal statuses: completed/failed/cancelled/interrupted with appropriate toasts.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (cancel button visible/clickable, toasts working; 2 minor race conditions remain)

seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_CANCEL pipeline runs created during backend tests (all cancelled/completed, none in 'running' state).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cancel pipeline fully tested and working. 3 frontend bugs fixed (auto-resume run_id→id, missing setPipelineAgentStatus on resume + pipeline start). Backend TOCTOU race fixed. 2 minor remaining: (1) double toast for interrupted if poll in-flight; (2) stale agent badges after cancel if poll in-flight. These can be fixed by adding clearInterval inside the polling callback when terminal status detected, and/or using a useRef cancel flag.

backend_toctou.bug: Final status check in _execute_server_pipeline used find_one + (if running) + update_one, creating a window where cancel API could set status='cancelled' between the read and write, then the stale 'running' read would cause update_one to set 'completed' (overwriting 'cancelled').
backend_toctou.fix: Replaced with atomic update_one({id, status:'running'}, {$set:{status:'completed'}}) - only updates if STILL running at write time.

frontend_auto_resume.bug: GET /api/pipeline/run/project/{id}/latest returns the DB document which has field 'id'. The POST /api/pipeline/run response has 'run_id'. Auto-resume code used latestRun.run_id (undefined on GET response), so activePipelineId was set to undefined → no polling → no cancel button.
